---
title: "Exploring the relationship between poverty and homicide rates from the Mexican War on Drugs"
format: html

This project is a lightly modified adaptation of my honors thesis in completion of the BA degree in Political Science at Pitzer College.



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(foreign)
#install.packages("gapminder")
library(gapminder)
library(readxl)
#install.packages("stargazer")
library(stargazer)

# importing dataset: homicides 
file.list <- list.files(pattern= '*.xlsx')
cidehomicide <- lapply(file.list, read_excel)
 
file.path <- "~/Users/diegoflores/Documents/RBSI-Working-directory"
file.list <- list.files(path = file.path, pattern = '*.xlsx') 


library(readxl)
file.list <- list.files(pattern='*.xlsx')
df.list <- lapply(file.list, read_excel)
cidehomicide4 <- bind_rows(df.list, .id = "id")
# cidehomicide4 is all xlsx files (2006-2011) in one df 

cidehomicide2010 <- read_excel("A-X10.xlsx")
# 2010 homicide data for cross section 

marginalization <- read_excel("Mexgov - Marginalization index State & muncipio (base).xlsx")
marginalization <- read_csv("Marginalization index 06anexo base.csv")
marginalization <- read_csv("Marginalization index 06anexo base.csv")
cidehomicide <- read_csv("CIDE homicide data (A-X10).csv")
esochomicide <- read_dta(file = "ESOC Mexico.drug.related.murders.dta")

# recode new INEGI variable 


# subsetting and merging 
marginsub2 <- marginalization[, c("state code", "municipality code", "municipality", "total population", "IM", "marginalization grade")] #subset margin data, 6 variables* rm(cidehomicide)
rm(marginsub)
rm(margin2)

homicidesub2 <- homicidesub[,c("idunico", "deaths_total","deaths_execution", "deaths_confront", "deaths_aggression")]
homicidesub <- esochomicide %>% filter(year == 2010) # subset/filter year 2010* 

mhmerge <- merge(marginsub2, homicidesub2, by.x = c("municipality code"), by.y = c("idunico")) #merged data by INEGI code* 
table(mhmerge$deaths_total)
sum(is.na(mhmerge$deaths_total)) 
mhmerge_rename <- rename(mhmerge, municip_code = "municipality code", state_code = "state code", marg_grade = "marginalization grade", margin_index = IM, total_pop = "total population") 
# renamed variables, new df

mhmerge = subset(mhmerge, select = -c())
#possibly subset to remove repeat columns, ignore 

# Visualization* 
p1 <- ggplot(data = mhmerge, mapping = aes(x = IM, y = deaths_total))
p1 <- p1 + geom_point()
p1 <- ggplot(data = mhmerge, mapping = aes(x = IM, y = deaths_total, size = "total population")) + geom_point() # adding size of dot w total pop*
# Warning message:Using size for a discrete variable is not advised, ignore  

p1 <- ggplot(data = mhmerge_rename, mapping = aes(x = margin_index, y = deaths_total, color = as.integer(state_code))) + geom_point() +
labs(x = "Marginalization Index", y = "Homicides", title = "Correlation Between Marginalization Levels and Drug War Homicides") +
  ylim(0, 750)

p1 <- ggplot(data = mhmerge_rename, mapping = aes(x = margin_index, y = deaths_total, color = as.integer(state_code))) + geom_point() + 
  labs(x = "Marginalization Index", y = "Homicides", 
       title = "Correlation Between Marginalization Levels and Drug War Homicides in 2010",
       subtitle = "Points are recorded homicides") + ylim(0, 750) 
#log produces various errors: introduced infinite values in continuous x axis
# removed 1258 rows #ignore this
p1
write.csv(mhmerge_rename, "/Users/diegoflores/Documents/RBSI-Working-directory/mateo.csv") 
#sharing data w mateo  
  

p1 <- ggplot(data = mhmerge_rename,
             mapping = aes(x = margin_index, y = homiciderates, color = region)) +    
               geom_point(size = 1) +                           
               labs(x = "Marginalization Index", 
                    y = "Homicide rate per 100,000", 
                    title = "Correlation Between Marginalization Levels and Drug War Homicides", 
                    subtitle = "Data points are homicide rates per 100,000 people",
                    caption = "Source: Princeton Empirical Studies of Conflict") + 
  ylim(0, 750)
p1
#this is the plot for mhmerge rename w deaths total not homicide rate per 100k, do this for mhmerge_nj
p1 <- ggplot(data = mhmerge_nj,
             mapping = aes(x = margin_index, y = homiciderates, color = region)) +    
  geom_point(size = 1) +                           
  labs(x = "Marginalization Index", 
       y = "Homicide rate per 100,000", 
       title = "Correlation Between Marginalization Levels and Drug War Homicides", 
       subtitle = "Data points are homicide rates per 100,000 people",
       caption = "Source: Princeton Empirical Studies of Conflict") + 
  ylim(0, 750)
p1


#transformations
mhmerge_rename$loghom <- log(mhmerge_rename$deaths_total + 1) #ignore this
mhmerge_rename$population <- as.numeric(gsub(" ", "", mhmerge_rename$total_pop, fixed = TRUE)) 
# transform total_pop to  to numeric
mhmerge_rename$homiciderates <- (mhmerge_rename$deaths_total / mhmerge_rename$population) * 100000 
#creating homiciderates per 100k for rename data

mhmerge_nj$population <- as.numeric(gsub(" ", "", mhmerge_nj$total_pop, fixed = TRUE)) 
#nj pop data now has numeric value
mhmerge_nj$homiciderates <- (mhmerge_nj$deaths_total / mhmerge_nj$population) * 100000  
#added homiciderates per100k variable to nj


 

# New variable - region
region <- list(northwest = c(02, 03, 18, 25, 26), north = c(08, 05, 10, 32, 24), northeast = c(19, 28),
central_west = c(06, 11, 14, 16, 01), south_central = c(09, 13, 15, 17, 21, 22, 29), gulf = c(27, 30), peninsula = c(04,23, 31)) 
rm(egion) 
mhmerge_rename$region <- "southcentral"
mhmerge_rename$region[mhmerge_rename$state_code == c(02, 03, 18, 25, 26)] <- "northwest"
mhmerge_rename$region[mhmerge_rename$state_code == c(08, 05, 10, 32, 24)] <-"north"
mhmerge_rename$region[mhmerge_rename$state_code == c(19, 28)] <- "north"
mhmerge_rename$region[mhmerge_rename$state_code == c(01, 06, 11, 14, 16)] <-  "central_west"
mhmerge_rename$region[mhmerge_rename$state_code == c(27, 30)] <- "gulf"
mhmerge_rename$region[mhmerge_rename$state_code == c(04,23, 31)] <- "peninsula"

# No Juarez 
mhmerge_nj <- subset(mhmerge_rename, municip_code != 8037) #no juarez outlier
```

```{.r .cell-code}
#Regression 
model1 <- lm(deaths_total ~ margin_index + as.factor(state_code), data = mhmerge_nj) #regression w total deaths
model2 <- lm(deaths_execution ~ margin_index, data = mhmerge_nj) #regression w deaths from execution
model3 <- lm(deaths_confront ~ margin_index, data = mhmerge_nj) #deaths from confrontation
model4 <- lm(deaths_aggression ~ margin_index, data = mhmerge_nj) #deaths from aggression 
summary(model1) 
stargazer(model1, model2, model3, model4, type = "text") 

model1 <- lm(homiciderates ~ margin_index + as.factor(state_code), data = mhmerge_nj)

regressiontable1 <- tbl_regression(model1) print(regressiontable)

stargazer(model1, out = "regression1.txt")
```
